For someone with mixed device usage and a tight 1-3 month timeline, a responsive web app makes the most sense—it'll work across devices without the complexity of native development, and I can always wrap it with PWA or React Native later if needed. I'd go with Next.js paired with Firebase or Supabase to handle authentication, database, and real-time chat functionality, then walk through the office-hour lock logic and break down the implementation roadmap into concrete steps.Okay, based on ni — saya rasa **web app (responsive)** adalah pilihan paling logik untuk kau sekarang. Sebab:

- Dengan campuran mobile & desktop user, web app responsive boleh cover both dalam satu codebase — tak payah buat dua projek berasingan
- Kau dah ada pengalaman code tapi baru nak buat app real, jadi web app lebih straightforward berbanding native mobile (kurang kerumitan platform-specific)
- Timeline 1-3 bulan realistic untuk web app MVP, tapi cukup tight kalau kau cuba native mobile app pulak
- Lepas web app jalan lancar, kau boleh convert jadi mobile app guna PWA (Progressive Web App) atau wrap dengan Capacitor — tak perlu rewrite dari kosong

**Cadangan stack (senang untuk chat app + auth):**
- **Next.js** — frontend + backend dalam satu framework, ramai tutorial, sesuai untuk kau yang belum banyak experience real app
- **Firebase** atau **Supabase** — handle authentication (kau dah ada login dari Gemini tu, boleh integrate), real-time database untuk chat, dan senang manage user roles (student/lecturer/parent)

**Roadmap kasar untuk MVP (1-3 bulan):**

1. **Minggu 1-2**: Setup project structure, integrate login page sedia ada, define user roles (student/lecturer/parent) dalam database
2. **Minggu 3-5**: Bina core chat feature — real-time messaging antara dua user (contoh: student ↔ lecturer dulu, simple version)
3. **Minggu 6-7**: Implement office hour logic — lecturer set jadual office hour, sistem check masa semasa, lock/unlock chat automatik
4. **Minggu 8-9**: Tambah role parent (mungkin parent boleh view/monitor chat, atau ada channel berasingan)
5. **Minggu 10-12**: Testing, polish UI, deploy

Nak saya bantu setup project structure dulu (contoh: Next.js + Firebase), atau kau nak saya check dulu code login page yang kau dah siapkan tu supaya boleh integrate terus?

Simply build LibXenoverse.lib and link it. Win32 builds of the tools are provided on the /bin/ directory. It's recommended to use the data folder inside of data2.cpk to override the files from data.cpk.

You can also integrate LibXenoverse into your own applications if you wish to add support for these files on a custom game engine, previewer, plugin, etc. Simply build LibXenoverse.lib and link it. Win32 builds of the tools are provided on the /bin/ directory. It's recommended to use the data folder inside of data2.cpk to override the files from data.cpk. It's much smaller and faster to repack than the main big data.cpk. Use the CPKPack tool to extract and repack Dragon Ball Xenoverse's CPK files. WARNING: Repacking data2.cpk with the uncompressed files seem to just crash the game at the moment for some reason. Try extracting the files first with extract-data2.bat (leaves the files compressed) and repack those instead. Opening an ESK file will add an skeleton to the current view. Opening EMD files will add meshes to the current view, as well as load its materials and textures automatically from the EMM/EMB files from the same directory.

content.text:
Folder / directory structure options and naming conventions for software projects - kriasoft/Folder-Structure-Conventions

Folder structure options and naming conventions for software projects · . ├── build # Compiled files (alternatively `dist`) ├── docs # Documentation files (alternatively `doc`) ├── src # Source files (alternatively `lib` or `app`) ├── test # Automated tests (alternatively `spec` or `tests`) ├── tools # Tools and utilities ├── LICENSE └── README.md · Use short lowercase names at least for the top-level files and folders except LICENSE, README.md · The actual source files of a software project are usually stored inside the src folder. Alternatively, you can put them into the lib (if you're developing a library), or into the app folder (if your application's source files are not supposed to be compiled). Samples: jQuery src, Node.js lib and src, D3.js src, AngularJS src, Adobe Brackets src, three.js src, Express lib, Socket.IO lib, Less.js lib, Redis src, Ace lib, Semantic UI src, Zepto.js src, Emscripten src, RethinkDB src, Bitcoin src, MongoDB src, Facebook React src, Rust src, ASP.NET 

Often it is beneficial to include some reference data into the project, such as Rich Text Format (RTF) documentation, which is usually stored into the docs or, less commonly, into the doc folder. . ├── ... ├── docs # Documentation files (alternatively `doc`) │ ├── TOC.md # Table of contents │ ├── faq.md # Frequently asked questions │ ├── misc.md # Miscellaneous information │ ├── usage.md # Getting started guide │ └── ... # etc. └── ... Samples: HTML5 Boilerplate docs, Backbone docs, three.js docs, GitLab doc, Underscore.js docs, Grunt docs, Emscripten docs, RequireJS docs, GitHub Hubot docs, Twitter Flight doc, Video.js docs, Bitcoin doc, MongoDB docs, libgit2 docs, Stylus docs, Gulp docs, Apache httpd docs, Windows Terminal doc, OpenTelemetry .NET docs ... If you want to share your work with others, please consider choosing an open source license and include the text of the license into your project.

The user is asking whether pushing code updates to GitHub after already deploying their app will affect the live version. I need to clarify what they mean by "publish" — whether they deployed to Firebase Hosting, an app store, or shared an APK — since the answer depends on that. I should explain in Malay that GitHub pushes won't automatically affect a deployed app unless there's a CI/CD pipeline connected.Bagus soalan ni — jawapannya **bergantung macam mana kau "publish" app tu semalam**. Boleh kau confirm, publish tu maksud:

1. **Build APK/build web dan upload/share terus** (contoh: `flutter build apk`, upload ke Play Store/TestFlight, atau host static file kat Firebase Hosting/Netlify manual) — kalau ni, **git push takkan disturb apa-apa**. GitHub repo tu hanya simpan source code, bukan app yang live tu. Push code baru = repo kau updated je, app yang orang guna sekarang tak berubah sehingga kau build & re-deploy semula.

2. **Ada CI/CD auto-deploy** (contoh: GitHub Actions setup untuk auto-deploy bila push ke `main`) — kalau ni yang kau setup, baru risiko sikit, sebab push baru boleh trigger auto build & terus update app live. Tapi biasanya untuk beginner project macam ni, kau takde setup ni melainkan kau specifically configure.

**Ringkasnya:** kalau kau just guna `flutter build` manual dan share fail tu terus (bukan auto-deploy), **push code error pun selamat** — sebab:
- Error dalam code (contoh typo) hanya block build/compile kau sendiri kat laptop, bukan app yang dah published tu
- GitHub cuma simpan **source code history**, app yang user download/guna tu adalah fail build yang berasingan

Jadi jangan risau — try push je, kalau ada error masa `git push` (bukan error dalam app), biasanya sebab authentication/branch issue je, senang fix. Cuba dulu dan share error message kalau ada.

## Setting dalam Firebase Console

Pergi ke [console.firebase.google.com](https://console.firebase.google.com) → pilih project `tuturedu-app` → check benda-benda ni:

**1. Authentication**
- Menu **Build → Authentication → Sign-in method**
- Pastikan **Email/Password** provider dah **Enable** (kalau login kau function dah, mungkin dah enable — tapi confirm balik)

**2. Firestore Database**
- Menu **Build → Firestore Database**
- Pastikan database dah **create** (kalau belum, klik "Create database", pilih mode **production**, pilih region — contoh `asia-southeast1` sebab kau di Malaysia)
- Confirm ada collection `users` dengan document untuk setiap user (uid, email, role, name)

**3. Firestore Rules**
- Dalam Firestore Database page, klik tab **Rules**
- Kau boleh either:
  - **Paste terus** content `firestore.rules` yang saya bagi tadi kat sini terus dalam console, klik **Publish**, ATAU

input.description: README.md draft for the TuturEdu project
input.file_text:
# TuturEdu 🎓

TuturEdu ialah platform perbualan (chat) antara **Student**, **Lecturer**, dan **Parent**, dibina menggunakan Flutter dan Firebase. Sistem ini mengehadkan sesi perbualan kepada **waktu pejabat (office hour)** sahaja, bagi memastikan sempadan yang jelas antara masa kerja dan masa peribadi pensyarah.

🔗 **Live demo:** [tuturedu-app.web.app](https://tuturedu-app.web.app/)

## ✨ Ciri-ciri (Features)

- 🔐 **Login & Role-based Access** — sistem mengenal pasti peranan pengguna (Student / Lecturer / Parent) selepas log masuk dan menghalakan ke dashboard masing-masing
- 💬 **Real-time Chat** — perbualan dikemas kini secara langsung menggunakan Cloud Firestore
- 🔍 **Cari Pensyarah** — student boleh cari dan mulakan perbualan dengan pensyarah pilihan
- ⏰ **Office Hour Lock** — chat automatik dikunci di luar waktu pejabat (Isnin–Jumaat, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM)
- 🔒 **Firestore Security Rules** — setiap perbualan hanya boleh diakses oleh peserta yang terlibat

## 🛠️ Tech Stack

- **Framework:** Flutter
- **Backend:** Firebase (Authentication, Cloud Firestore, Hosting)
- **Bahasa:** Dart

## 📂 Struktur Project

```
lib/
├── main.dart                  # Entry point aplikasi
├── firebase_options.dart      # Konfigurasi Firebase (auto-generated)
├── models/
│   └── user_model.dart        # Model data pengguna
├── screens/
│   ├── login_screen.dart      # Skrin log masuk
│   ├── student_dashboard.dart
│   ├── lecturer_dashboard.dart
│   ├── parent_dashboard.dart
│   ├── lecturer_list_screen.dart  # Student cari & pilih pensyarah
│   └── chat_screen.dart       # Skrin perbualan real-time
└── utils/
    └── office_hours.dart      # Logik semakan waktu pejabat
```

## 🚀 Cara Setup (Development)

1. **Clone repo ini**
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/XenoRsx/TuturEdu.git
   cd TuturEdu
   ```

2. **Install dependencies**
   ```bash
   flutter pub get
   ```

3. **Setup Firebase**
   - Pastikan kau ada akses ke Firebase project `tuturedu-app`, atau setup project Firebase sendiri
   - Run `flutterfire configure` untuk generate `firebase_options.dart`

4. **Run aplikasi**
   ```bash
   flutter run
   ```

## 🔥 Firestore

### Struktur Data

```
users (collection)
  └── {uid}
        ├── uid
        ├── email
        ├── name
        └── role: "Student" | "Lecturer" | "Parent"

chats (collection)
  └── {chatId}
        ├── participants: [uid1, uid2]
        ├── lastMessage
        ├── lastUpdated
        └── messages (sub-collection)
              └── {messageId}
                    ├── senderId
                    ├── text
                    └── timestamp
```

### Deploy Security Rules

```bash
firebase deploy --only "firestore:rules"
```

## 🌐 Deploy (Web)

```bash
flutter build web
firebase deploy --only hosting
```

## 📌 Status Pembangunan

- [x] Login & role-based routing
- [x] Firebase Authentication + Firestore integration
- [x] Chat real-time antara student & lecturer
- [x] Office hour lock logic
- [x] Firestore security rules
- [ ] Register screen (self sign-up)
- [ ] Notification untuk mesej baru
- [ ] Chat list (senarai perbualan aktif)
- [ ] Modul Parent (monitoring / chat dengan lecturer)

## 👤 Dibina oleh

Dibangunkan sebagai projek peribadi/akademik menggunakan Flutter & Firebase.

input.file_text:
# TuturEdu

TuturEdu is a chat platform connecting Students, Lecturers, and Parents, built using Flutter and Firebase. The system restricts conversations to office hours only, ensuring a clear boundary between working hours and personal time for lecturers.

This project is developed as a Final Year Project (FYP) at Politeknik Metro Tasek Gelugor.

Live demo: https://tuturedu-app.web.app/

## Features

- Login & Role-based Access — the system identifies user roles (Student / Lecturer / Parent) after login and routes them to their respective dashboards
- Real-time Chat — conversations update live using Cloud Firestore
- Find Lecturer — students can search for and start a conversation with a lecturer
- Office Hour Lock — chat is automatically locked outside office hours (Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM)
- Firestore Security Rules — each conversation can only be accessed by its participants

## Tech Stack

- Framework: Flutter
- Backend: Firebase (Authentication, Cloud Firestore, Hosting)
- Language: Dart

## Project Structure

```
lib/
├── main.dart                  # App entry point
├── firebase_options.dart      # Firebase configuration (auto-generated)
├── models/
│   └── user_model.dart        # User data model
├── screens/
│   ├── login_screen.dart      # Login screen
│   ├── student_dashboard.dart
│   ├── lecturer_dashboard.dart
│   ├── parent_dashboard.dart
│   ├── lecturer_list_screen.dart  # Student search & select lecturer
│   └── chat_screen.dart       # Real-time chat screen
└── utils/
    └── office_hours.dart      # Office hour check logic
```

## Firestore

### Data Structure

```
users (collection)
  └── {uid}
        ├── uid
        ├── email
        ├── name
        └── role: "Student" | "Lecturer" | "Parent"

chats (collection)
  └── {chatId}
        ├── participants: [uid1, uid2]
        ├── lastMessage
        ├── lastUpdated
        └── messages (sub-collection)
              └── {messageId}
                    ├── senderId
                    ├── text
                    └── timestamp
```

### Deploy Security Rules

```bash
firebase deploy --only "firestore:rules"
```

## Deploy (Web)

```bash
flutter build web
firebase deploy --only hosting
```

## Development Status

- [x] Login & role-based routing
- [x] Firebase Authentication + Firestore integration
- [x] Real-time chat between student & lecturer
- [x] Office hour lock logic
- [x] Firestore security rules
- [ ] Register screen (self sign-up)
- [ ] Notifications for new messages
- [ ] Chat list (active conversations overview)
- [ ] Parent module (monitoring / chat with lecturer)

## Author

Developed as a Final Year Project (FYP) at Politeknik Metro Tasek Gelugor, using Flutter & Firebase.

TuturEdu is a chat platform connecting Students, Lecturers, and Parents, built using Flutter and Firebase. The system restricts conversations to office hours only, ensuring a clear boundary between working hours and personal time for lecturers.

This project is developed as a Final Year Project (FYP) at Politeknik Metro Tasek Gelugor.

Live demo: https://tuturedu-app.web.app/

## Features

- Login & Role-based Access — the system identifies user roles (Student / Lecturer / Parent) after login and routes them to their respective dashboards
- Real-time Chat — conversations update live using Cloud Firestore
- Find Lecturer — students can search for and start a conversation with a lecturer
- Office Hour Lock — chat is automatically locked outside office hours (Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM)
- Firestore Security Rules — each conversation can only be accessed by its participants

## Tech Stack

- Framework: Flutter
- Backend: Firebase (Authentication, Cloud Firestore, Hosting)
- Language: Dart

## Project Structure

```
lib/
├── main.dart                  # App entry point
├── firebase_options.dart      # Firebase configuration (auto-generated)
├── models/
│   └── user_model.dart        # User data model
├── screens/
│   ├── login_screen.dart      # Login screen
│   ├── student_dashboard.dart
│   ├── lecturer_dashboard.dart
│   ├── parent_dashboard.dart
│   ├── lecturer_list_screen.dart  # Student search & select lecturer
│   └── chat_screen.dart       # Real-time chat screen
└── utils/
    └── office_hours.dart      # Office hour check logic
```

## Firestore

### Data Structure

```
users (collection)
  └── {uid}
        ├── uid
        ├── email
        ├── name
        └── role: "Student" | "Lecturer" | "Parent"

chats (collection)
  └── {chatId}
        ├── participants: [uid1, uid2]
        ├── lastMessage
        ├── lastUpdated
        └── messages (sub-collection)
              └── {messageId}
                    ├── senderId
                    ├── text
                    └── timestamp
```

### Deploy Security Rules

```bash
firebase deploy --only "firestore:rules"
```

## Deploy (Web)

```bash
flutter build web
firebase deploy --only hosting
```

## Development Status

- [x] Login & role-based routing
- [x] Firebase Authentication + Firestore integration
- [x] Real-time chat between student & lecturer
- [x] Office hour lock logic
- [x] Firestore security rules
- [ ] Register screen (self sign-up)
- [ ] Notifications for new messages
- [ ] Chat list (active conversations overview)
- [ ] Parent module (monitoring / chat with lecturer)

## Screen-screen utama (Lecturer)
- **Home/Dashboard** — status On-Duty/Off-Duty toggle, search, filter (All/Class/Official/Parents), active conversations list
- **Settings** — On-Duty status toggle, working hours setting (8AM-5PM), quiet mode, push notifications, attendance alert, change password
- **Profile** — info pensyarah, subjek yang diajar, quick stats (attendance %, jumlah student)
- **Class Performance Overview** — health score, breakdown per student (%, trend: Steady/Dropping/Critical), button "Send Warning Letter"
- **Off-Duty Chat** — UI bila chat locked, ada banner "Outside Office Hours Guard", butang **"Reply Now (Overtime Mode)"** dan **"Schedule Reply for 8AM"**
- **On-Duty Chat** — chat aktif, quick reply chips (OK/Thank you/Wait), attachment (document/image/audio)

## Screen-screen Student
- Sama struktur — Home, Settings, Profile (+ ada **Parent Info** section dalam profile student), Attendance Overview (dengan status warning kalau attendance rendah), Off-Duty/On-Duty chat

- Profile khas untuk parent — nampak anak dia punya info, attendance, dan chat dengan lecturer

3. **Quick reply chips** dalam chat (OK/Thank you/Wait)

content.text:
Wah, comprehensive gila prototype Figma kau ni! Saya dah scan struktur penuh — ada **Lecturer flow** dan **Student flow** yang lengkap (nampak macam duplicate/versi untuk both role), plus modul untuk Parent. Ni ringkasan apa yang saya jumpa:

## Screen-screen utama (Lecturer)
- **Home/Dashboard** — status On-Duty/Off-Duty toggle, search, filter (All/Class/Official/Parents), active conversations list
- **Settings** — On-Duty status toggle, working hours setting (8AM-5PM), quiet mode, push notifications, attendance alert, change password
- **Profile** — info pensyarah, subjek yang diajar, quick stats (attendance %, jumlah student)
- **Class Performance Overview** — health score, breakdown per student (%, trend: Steady/Dropping/Critical), button "Send Warning Letter"
- **Off-Duty Chat** — UI bila chat locked, ada banner "Outside Office Hours Guard", butang **"Reply Now (Overtime Mode)"** dan **"Schedule Reply for 8AM"**
- **On-Duty Chat** — chat aktif, quick reply chips (OK/Thank you/Wait), attachment (document/image/audio)

## Screen-screen Student
- Sama struktur — Home, Settings, Profile (+ ada **Parent Info** section dalam profile student), Attendance Overview (dengan status warning kalau attendance rendah), Off-Duty/On-Duty chat

## Modul Parent
- Profile khas untuk parent — nampak anak dia punya info, attendance, dan chat dengan lecturer

---

**Ni jauh lebih advanced dari apa yang kita dah code setakat ni!** Beberapa feature besar dalam design yang belum ada dalam code:

1. **Overtime Mode** — lecturer boleh reply walaupun outside office hour (override), atau schedule reply untuk esok
2. **Class Performance / Attendance tracking** — dengan health score & warning letter system
3. **Quick reply chips** dalam chat (OK/Thank you/Wait)
4. **Attachment support** (document/image/audio)
5. **On-Duty/Off-Duty manual toggle** oleh lecturer (bukan just automatic ikut jadual)

Nak saya screenshot beberapa screen penting dulu (contoh Off-Duty chat & Home dashboard) supaya kau boleh confirm visual tepat, atau terus kita bincang mana feature nak prioritize untuk implement dulu?
